channel.title: Drupal Core
channel.description: Stay informed about developments in Drupal Core.

item.title: #2258355: Deprecate hide() and show()
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 4 files, +65/−30 · php +65/−30
  • Diff: afe536a
  • Discussion: #2258355 · 25 contributors · 131 comments
  • Followers: 25

Summary

The global procedural functions hide() and show() defined in core/includes/common.inc are deprecated as of Drupal 10.1.0 and will be removed in Drupal 11.0.0. Code that calls these functions will trigger a deprecation notice. The replacement is to set the render array property directly: use $element['#printed'] = TRUE in place of hide($element), and $element['#printed'] = FALSE in place of show($element). The one core usage of these functions, in the file field preprocess function, has been updated to use the direct property assignment. The conceptual documentation about how the #printed flag controls rendering traversal has been moved from the individual function docblocks into RendererInterface::render(), where it more accurately belongs.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • Deprecation: Global hide() and show() functions deprecated in drupal:10.1.0 and removed in drupal:11.0.0; replace with direct $element['#printed'] = TRUE/FALSE assignments.

Technical details

Both hide() and show() in core/includes/common.inc simply flip the #printed boolean property on a render array element. The #printed flag acts as a semaphore: when TRUE, the renderer skips that element and its children on subsequent render passes. Because the functions are nothing more than thin wrappers around a one-line property assignment, the deprecation has no replacement function; callers are directed to inline the assignment. The @trigger_error() call in each function points to change record https://www.drupal.org/node/3261271.

The docblock explaining the #printed caching and traversal semantics (the "first time render() is called" explanation) was stripped from the two deprecated functions and added to RendererInterface::render() in core/lib/Drupal/Core/Render/RendererInterface.php, since that is where the behavior is actually implemented.

The sole core callsite, template_preprocess_file_widget_multiple() in core/modules/file/file.field.inc, was updated to use $widget[$sub_key]['#printed'] = TRUE and $widget['display']['#printed'] = TRUE in place of hide(), and $operations_elements[$key]['#printed'] = FALSE in place of show(). A new unit test class ProceduralApiDeprecationTest in core/tests/Drupal/Tests/Core/Render/Element/ verifies both deprecation messages fire and that the functions still return the expected array shapes during the transition window.

Upgrade

Replace every call to the global hide() and show() functions with a direct property assignment:

// Before
hide($element['some_child']);
show($element['some_child']);

// After
$element['some_child']['#printed'] = TRUE;
$element['some_child']['#printed'] = FALSE;

Note: if you are hiding a form element to remove it from output, using $element['#access'] = FALSE is often more appropriate, as it also prevents form submission of that element.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 5, 2014
  • Committed: May 7, 2026 (12 years and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • marcingy
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)
  • Arnion (Skilld)
  • andypost (Skilld)
  • MerryHamster (Skilld)
  • karishmaamin (Specbee)
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)
  • nnevill (DevBranch)
  • voleger (Drupal Ukraine Community)
  • nod_ (Acquia)
  • bhanu951
  • spokje
  • xjm
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • catch (Third and Grove)
  • vladimiraus (Tomato Elephant Studio)
  • bnjmnm (Acquia)

Collaboration

longwave raised a substantive design question late in the review: the original plan was to move hide() and show() to static methods on \Drupal\Core\Render\Element, but longwave noted that with Twig and its without() filter now covering the themer use case, and most contrib usage actually being form-element hiding that should use #access instead, there was no strong reason to keep the methods as public API at all. andypost picked up the point, updated the MR to deprecate without replacement, and moved the #printed traversal documentation to RendererInterface::render(). The shift from "move to a class" to "deprecate with no replacement" emerged directly from this exchange.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3573954: Move common code across Drupal base test suite classes to DrupalTestCaseTrait
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: main
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 12 files, +75/−87 · php +75/−87
  • Diff: fe682be
  • Discussion: #3573954 · 5 contributors · 24 comments
  • Followers: 7

Summary

Three symbols used in custom and contrib test classes are deprecated in Drupal 12 and will be removed in Drupal 13: TestRequirementsTrait, its getDrupalRoot() method, and BrowserTestBase::setUpAppRoot(). The underlying functionality (resolving the Drupal root path and registering the debug dump handler) has been consolidated into DrupalTestCaseTrait, which all Drupal base test classes already use. Developers whose test code references any of the deprecated symbols will start seeing deprecation notices and must update before upgrading to Drupal 13.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • Deprecation: TestRequirementsTrait, TestRequirementsTrait::getDrupalRoot(), and BrowserTestBase::setUpAppRoot() deprecated in drupal:12.0.0 and removed in drupal:13.0.0; use DrupalTestCaseTrait and access $this->root directly.

Technical details

The consolidation was motivated by three identical copies of setDebugDumpHandler() (a #[BeforeClass] hook registering the VarDumper CLI handler) and two copies of getDrupalRoot() living independently in KernelTestBase, BrowserTestBase, and UnitTestCase. Both methods, plus the $root property previously declared redundantly in those classes and in TestSetupTrait, are now defined once in DrupalTestCaseTrait.

The $root property in DrupalTestCaseTrait is declared with a PHP 8.4 property hook for lazy initialization: the get hook calls static::getDrupalRoot() on first access and caches the result, so explicit setUp() assignments in subclasses are no longer needed. Because property hooks require PHP 8.4, this change targets the main branch (Drupal 12) only. A phpcs:ignoreFile directive was added to DrupalTestCaseTrait temporarily because PHPCS does not yet parse hooked property syntax.

TestRequirementsTrait is deprecated at the file level via a top-of-file @trigger_error, its getDrupalRoot() method carries an individual deprecation notice, and BrowserTestBase::setUpAppRoot() is deprecated with a notice to access $this->root directly. The change record is at https://www.drupal.org/node/3574112.

Upgrade

If your test class uses TestRequirementsTrait, remove it. All Drupal base test classes (UnitTestCase, KernelTestBase, BrowserTestBase) already pull the same functionality from DrupalTestCaseTrait.

// Before
use Drupal\Tests\TestRequirementsTrait;

class MyTest extends UnitTestCase {
  use TestRequirementsTrait;
  ...
}

// After — no use statement needed; functionality comes from UnitTestCase
class MyTest extends UnitTestCase {
  ...
}

If your test code calls static::getDrupalRoot(), access $this->root instead:

// Before
$root = static::getDrupalRoot();

// After
$root = $this->root;

If your test class overrides or calls setUpAppRoot(), remove the call:

// Before (in setUp())
$this->setUpAppRoot();

// After — remove the call; $this->root is initialized lazily via the property hook

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: February 17, 2026
  • Committed: May 7, 2026 (2 months later)

Key contributors

  • mondrake
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • dcam
  • quietone (PreviousNext)
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3066751: Add resolvable_uri property to LinkItem for APIs
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 8 files, +196/−10 · php +196/−10
  • Diff: ff86b9f
  • Discussion: #3066751 · 27 contributors · 106 comments
  • Followers: 48

Summary

Link fields exposed through JSON:API and REST previously returned only the internal URI format (such as entity:node/5 or internal:/node/add), which decoupled front-end applications cannot use directly to render links. A new computed property, resolvable_uri, is now included in link field output alongside the existing uri, title, and options properties. It contains the processed, routable URL: relative paths for internal links (for example /node/5) and full absolute URLs for external links. The property can also be used to set a link field item's value in PHP, with the internal uri and options being derived automatically.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New computed resolvable_uri property on LinkItem (and new LinkResolvableUriComputed DataType plugin) that returns the processed, routable URL for a link field item; appears in JSON:API and REST output and can be written to in PHP.

Technical details

A new DataType plugin, LinkResolvableUriComputed (plugin id resolvable_uri), is registered in core/modules/link/src/Plugin/DataType/LinkResolvableUriComputed.php. It extends Uri and implements CacheableDependencyInterface. Its getValue() method calls $item->getUrl()->toString(TRUE) on the parent LinkItem and caches the resulting GeneratedUrl; the getCacheTags(), getCacheContexts(), and getCacheMaxAge() methods delegate to that same object so cacheability metadata is correctly propagated.

LinkItem::propertyDefinitions() registers resolvable_uri as a computed, non-internal, read-only property using this data type. LinkItem::onChange() is added to keep the stored properties in sync when resolvable_uri is written directly: it parses the incoming URL, prepends the internal: scheme when the path contains no scheme marker, and calls writePropertyValue() for uri and optionally options. LinkItem::setValue() is updated to call onChange('resolvable_uri', FALSE) when the value array contains a resolvable_uri key but no uri key, enabling the field to be set entirely through the new property (for example $entity->field_link = ['resolvable_uri' => '/node/add']).

The property name went through two earlier iterations. url was blocked because Twig resolves field.0.url as a call to LinkItem::getUrl(), shadowing the method and silently returning NULL for any chained access like field.0.url.routename. The name was changed to full_url in community patches that were widely deployed, then finally to resolvable_uri in the MR that landed.

Upgrade

No action is required for sites that have not applied earlier community patches. Sites or front-end applications that relied on the url or full_url property from unofficial patches must rename all references to resolvable_uri.

// Old (patch-era, never in a Drupal core release):
$entity->field_link->url
$entity->field_link->full_url

// New (Drupal core, 11.x / main):
$entity->field_link->resolvable_uri

// Setting a link field item via the new property:
$entity->field_link->resolvable_uri = 'https://www.drupal.org?test_param=value#main-content';

// Or in an array assignment (uri is derived automatically):
$entity->field_link = ['resolvable_uri' => '/node/add'];

JSON:API and REST responses will now include resolvable_uri in every link field item. Any client code that deserializes or validates link field shapes may need to be updated to accept the new key.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: July 9, 2019
  • Committed: May 7, 2026 (6 years and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • justafish (Lullabot)
  • jibran (PreviousNext)
  • lauriii (Acquia)
  • duozersk (Five Jars)
  • jonnyeom
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)
  • vsujeetkumar (Material)
  • andregp (CI&T)
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • wim leers (Acquia)
  • greg__
  • kristen pol (Itty Bitty Byte)
  • nitesh sethia (Digital Polygon)
  • dcam
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)

Collaboration

The property name produced a significant design dispute. An early reviewer (greg__) reported in comment #22 that the original name url silently broke Twig templates: field.0.url.routename resolves in Twig to $item->getUrl()->getRouteName(), so the new computed property shadowed the existing method and returned NULL for any chained access. jibran and lauriii confirmed the conflict. alexpott explicitly blocked commitment in comment #45, pointing to contrib templates already using the pattern and stating "This looks disruptive in its current form." The issue sat at RTBC for over a month before that review.

The name was changed to full_url and community patches under that name were widely deployed for years. When dcam reopened the MR in 2025 targeting 11.x, he renamed the property to resolvable_uri, citing semantic clarity. dgroene objected in comment #98 that yet another rename was painful for sites already running full_url in production. Committer longwave acknowledged the history but agreed that full_url was not sufficiently descriptive and committed under the resolvable_uri name, noting minor typing issues would be cleaned up separately rather than holding the issue any longer.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3579661: Call to a member function getPath() on null in EntityRouteHelper->isContentEntityRoute()
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 11.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +11/−3 · php +11/−3
  • Diff: c683664
  • Discussion: #3579661 · 6 contributors · 20 comments
  • Followers: 8

Summary

Sites with the Navigation module enabled crashed with a fatal error when any non-existent URL was visited. The error surfaced during page rendering while the Navigation module tried to determine whether the current route is a content-entity route, and the route object was null for 404 pages. This fix prevents the crash so 404 pages render normally. The patch was committed to main, 11.x, and 11.3.x.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

EntityRouteHelper::isContentEntityRoute() called $this->routeMatch->getRouteObject()->getPath() without first checking whether getRouteObject() returns a non-null value. On a 404 route the route object is null, causing the fatal Call to a member function getPath() on null. The call chain was: TopBar::preRenderTopBar -> PageActions::build -> NavigationRenderer::hasLocalTasks -> NavigationRenderer::getLocalTasks -> EntityRouteHelper::isContentEntityRoute.

The fix introduces an early if (!$route) { return FALSE; } guard, consistent with the pattern already applied to getContentEntityFromRoute() in the same class (see #3565886, which fixed the same class for a related method but missed isContentEntityRoute()). A kernel test assertion for isContentEntityRoute() returning FALSE on a non-existent route was added to the existing NavigationEntityRouteHelperTest.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 17, 2026
  • Committed: May 6, 2026 (1 month and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • kevinvb
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • sivaji_ganesh_jojodae (Material)
  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • catch (Third and Grove)

Collaboration

penyaskito noted they had encountered the same error while working on Drupal CMS and adjusted the patch: they renamed the kernel test to cover both methods and changed the fix to match the approach already used in getContentEntityFromRoute(), making the two methods consistent. smustgrave verified the test reproduced the error and confirmed LGTM.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3570634: Remove fallback classloader and related code in AttributeClassDiscovery
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: main
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 5 files, +18/−191 · php +18/−191
  • Diff: 15d0117
  • Discussion: #3570634 · 3 contributors · 16 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

The temporary workaround that let plugin attribute discovery handle missing traits without a fatal error has been removed from AttributeClassDiscovery. The workaround was introduced in #3502913 to paper over a PHP limitation where loading a class that uses a non-existent trait caused an unrecoverable fatal error. PHP 8.5 fixes this by making the same situation throw a catchable \Error, so the workaround is no longer needed. The practical effect for most sites is nothing: plugins whose classes depend on uninstalled modules continue to be silently skipped during discovery, just through simpler code.

Impact

Affects site owners.

  • Requirements: Relies on PHP 8.5 behavior where loading a class with a missing trait throws a catchable \Error; this removal is only safe because Drupal main now targets PHP 8.5.

Technical details

The removed workaround registered MissingClassDetectionClassLoader as an spl_autoload handler during every getDefinitions() call. When PHP tried to load a class with a missing trait, this handler fired last, recorded the missing trait in $missingTraits, and aliased it to StubTrait so reflection could proceed. AttributeClassDiscovery tracked affected classes in a static $skipClasses array and re-checked them on subsequent discovery passes, comparing the missing trait's two-level namespace against registered plugin namespaces to decide whether to allow or skip the class. Plugins with missing traits were also excluded from the file cache so they would be re-scanned on every request.

With PHP 8.5, class_exists() on a class that uses a non-existent trait throws a catchable \Error. The diff deletes MissingClassDetectionClassLoader and StubTrait entirely, removes the spl_autoload_register / spl_autoload_unregister calls, drops the $skipClasses static property and all associated re-check logic, and reduces the error-handling block inside getDefinitions() to a bare catch (\Error) { continue; }. File cache writes for successfully resolved plugins are now unconditional.

The one visible behavioral difference, captured in the updated AttributeClassDiscoveryCachedTest::testGetDefinitionsMissingTrait(), is that if discovery runs twice in a single request and the missing trait's module is installed between the two passes, the plugin is no longer re-discovered in the second pass. Previously the stub-alias meant PHP would not throw again, so the class appeared valid. Now PHP 8.5 throws an \Error on every class_exists() call for that class until the next full discovery cycle. The thread also considered restoring a preg_match('/(Class|Interface|Trait) .* not found$/', $e->getMessage()) guard to prevent silently swallowing unexpected \Error exceptions, but contributors decided against it as fragile given how unlikely such exceptions are in practice; the tracking issue #3520811 is expected to be closed as won't-do.

Upgrade

Both removed classes were @internal and not part of Drupal's public API. If you referenced them despite that marker, remove those references:

// Remove these imports and any usage:
use Drupal\Component\Discovery\MissingClassDetectionClassLoader;
use Drupal\Component\Discovery\StubTrait;

If your code installs a module and then calls getDefinitions() again in the same request expecting to find a previously-missing-trait plugin, that plugin will no longer appear until the next request's discovery cycle. Move such code to a fresh request or clear the plugin cache after module installation.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: January 30, 2026
  • Committed: May 6, 2026 (3 months later)

Collaboration

catch, who wrote the original MissingClassDetectionClassLoader workaround in #3502913, remarked: "Was pleased with myself when I got this working in the original issue, but even more pleased to get rid of it as soon as humanly possible," and at merge time called it "a horrible hack." godotislate raised a substantive concern: removing the classloader without restoring the earlier preg_match guard means any unexpected \Error from class_exists() would be silently swallowed. catch and godotislate weighed this against the brittleness of matching on exception message strings and concluded the risk was acceptable, pointing to #3520811 as the place to track that question.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3587182: Update CKEditor 5 to 48.0.1
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Critical task
  • Version: main
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 4 files, +31/−31 · yml +26/−26 · php +2/−2 · js +2/−2 · json +1/−1
  • Diff: 30521cd
  • Discussion: #3587182 · 6 contributors · 22 comments
  • Followers: 7

Summary

CKEditor 5 has been updated from 47.6.0 to 48.0.1 in Drupal core's main branch (Drupal 12 only; Drupal 11 stays on the CKEditor 5 v47 LTS line per an agreement with CKSource). The new version resolves long-standing HTML table problems: the editor no longer collapses multiple <tbody> sections or strips <thead>, which had impeded screen-reader accessibility. Support for <tfoot> and correct scope attributes on header cells is also included. This upgrade was blocked until the switch away from the DLL installation format landed in #3527914.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The version strings in core/assets/vendor/ckeditor5/ckeditor5.libraries.yml were bumped from 47.6.0 to 48.0.1 across every sub-library entry (the per-plugin libraries such as ckeditor5.table remain present but are already deprecated for removal in 13.0.0). The npm devDependency in core/package.json was updated from the ~47.6.0 range to ^48.0.1. CKEditor 5 v48 dropped the DLL build format entirely, so #3527914 (switching to the UMD installation method) was a hard prerequisite for this upgrade.

The core/scripts/js/jsdoc-generate.js script was also fixed for compatibility with v48's distribution layout: the glob was changed from ./ckeditor5*/src/**/*.+(js|jsdoc) to ./ckeditor5*/dist/**/*.d.ts (TypeScript declaration files), and the export-matching regex was extended to recognise declare, interface, const, type, and enum in addition to class and function. This was prompted by a review comment from godotislate noting the script produced an empty output against the v48 package structure; fixing it also allowed closing #3551053 as "won't fix".

The AssetAggregationAcrossPagesTest byte thresholds were updated to reflect the slightly smaller bundle produced by v48: ScriptBytes dropped from 3,222,537 to 3,206,361 and StylesheetBytes from 1,386,351 to 1,286,838.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 27, 2026
  • Committed: May 6, 2026 (8 days later)

Key contributors

  • joseph.olstad (Entreprise 7pro.ca Inc)
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)
  • godotislate
  • andrew.wang (Ontario Digital Service)
  • catch (Third and Grove)

Collaboration

When the issue was filed, longwave immediately flagged that CKEditor 5 v48 had dropped DLL support, making the upgrade impossible until #3527914 (the UMD installation-method switch) landed, and noted that Drupal 11 would deliberately stay on the v47 LTS line. Once that prerequisite merged, longwave opened the MR. During review godotislate observed that the jsdoc generation script was silently producing empty output against v48's changed package layout; longwave identified that v48 ships TypeScript .d.ts files instead of JS source files, updated the glob and regex accordingly, and closed the separate tracking issue #3551053 as no longer needed. After an initial RTBC, a merge conflict caused the asset-byte test to fail; godotislate rebased, updated the byte thresholds, and moved the issue back to RTBC for final commit.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3574506: Sorting by Views result counter causes ORDER BY unknown
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Major bug report
  • Version: 11.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +8/−0 · php +8
  • Diff: 1a1999d
  • Discussion: #3574506 · 6 contributors · 29 comments
  • Followers: 8

Summary

If you have a Views table display that includes the "Views result counter" (row number) field and that column was made click-sortable, clicking the column header produced a fatal database error: ORDER BY "unknown". The counter field has no underlying database column to sort on, so the generated SQL was invalid. This is now fixed. The counter field is explicitly marked as non-sortable, so the sort option no longer appears in the table header. A cache rebuild is triggered automatically on update. Note that existing view configuration that already has this column marked as sortable is not automatically corrected; a follow-up issue (#3588188) tracks that cleanup.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The root cause is that the "Views result counter" field is a computed pseudo-field with no real database column. When a column in a Views table has no click sortable constraint set, Views falls back to the literal string "unknown" as the ORDER BY value, which MySQL rejects as an invalid column reference.

The fix adds 'click sortable' => FALSE to the counter field's entry in the viewsData() method of the Views counter field plugin. This single key prevents Views from offering or executing click-sort on this field.

Because Views caches its field data definitions, an empty views_post_update_counter_field_not_sortable() hook is added to views.post_update.php. The empty hook triggers a cache rebuild on drush updb / update.php so that the corrected field definition is picked up by existing sites without requiring a manual cache clear. The post-update hook was required even for a patch release because alexpott noted the Views data cache must be invalidated for the fix to take effect on running installations.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: February 19, 2026
  • Committed: May 5, 2026 (2 months and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • mike-michal
  • quietone (PreviousNext)
  • lendude (iO)
  • sivaji_ganesh_jojodae
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)

Collaboration

During review, lendude argued that cleaning up existing view configuration was unnecessary because the format settings "are a mess anyway" and the click-sort option would disappear from the UI even without a config fix. alexpott pushed back, clarifying that the view configuration is only repaired if an editor revisits the table settings and saves, and that a post-update hook (even an empty one) was needed to rebuild the Views data cache for the fix to land in a patch release. alexpott filed #3588188 to handle the deeper config correction in a minor release, then committed the fix.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3569096: Remove BC layer $options argument from Constraint plugin constructors
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: main
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 79 files, +310/−587 · php +310/−587
  • Diff: 8447a07
  • Discussion: #3569096 · 4 contributors · 15 comments
  • Followers: 4

Summary

In preparation for Symfony 8 compatibility in Drupal 12, the legacy $options backward-compatibility argument has been removed from the constructors of all built-in Drupal constraint plugin classes. At the same time, the getDefaultOption() and getRequiredOptions() methods, deprecated in Symfony 7.4 and removed in Symfony 8, have been deleted from every Drupal constraint class that previously overrode them. This change is internal to the validation subsystem and has no user-facing effect, but it does break any custom module that either subclasses a built-in constraint or instantiates one using the old positional-array $options calling convention.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • API change: The mixed $options = NULL first constructor parameter is removed from all built-in Drupal constraint classes; getDefaultOption() and getRequiredOptions() overrides are also removed, mirroring their removal in Symfony 8.

Technical details

Symfony's Constraint base class historically accepted a catch-all mixed $options first argument that let callers pass constraint properties as a positional array. Symfony deprecated this pattern in 7.4 and removes it entirely in Symfony 8. Each affected Drupal constraint class previously forwarded that argument to parent::__construct($options, $groups, $payload).

The change removes mixed $options = NULL from the constructor of every Drupal-defined constraint class (roughly 50 classes spanning core/, ckeditor5/, file/, link/, media/, user/, and several test modules). parent::__construct() calls are updated to use named arguments: parent::__construct(groups: $groups, payload: $payload). Properties that were previously declared as class-level public $foo = default and then assigned conditionally in the constructor body (the $this->foo = $foo ?? $this->foo pattern) are collapsed into constructor property promotion. Where a property was previously "required" only through getRequiredOptions() but had a nullable default, the constructor parameter becomes genuinely required with no default (e.g., BundleConstraint::$bundle, EntityHasFieldConstraint::$field_name, ImmutablePropertiesConstraint::$properties, ValidKeysConstraint::$allowedKeys, StringEqualsConcatenatedValuesConstraint::$separator and $values, and others).

ComplexDataConstraint required special handling because it previously relied on the $options array to accept a bare array of properties without a properties key. That implicit-key logic is replaced with an explicit check: if $properties is NULL but variadic $otherProperties are present, those become the properties; otherwise an \InvalidArgumentException is thrown. MenuLinkDepthConstraint, which extended Symfony's RangeConstraint and forwarded arguments via ...$args, is rewritten to explicitly enumerate all parent constructor parameters. EmailConstraint and RangeConstraint (Drupal subclasses of Symfony built-ins) pass NULL explicitly as the first argument rather than forwarding a Drupal-level $options param.

The companion issue #3569092 covers the related removal of HasNamedArguments and the ConstraintManager::create() / addConstraint() signature updates.

Upgrade

Custom constraint plugins that subclass any of the affected classes, or that follow the same pattern, must be updated:

  1. Remove the $options parameter from your constructor:
// Before
public function __construct(
    mixed $options = NULL,
    public string $myOption = 'default',
    ?array $groups = NULL,
    mixed $payload = NULL,
) {
    parent::__construct($options, $groups, $payload);
    $this->myOption = $myOption ?? $this->myOption;
}

// After
public function __construct(
    public string $myOption = 'default',
    ?array $groups = NULL,
    mixed $payload = NULL,
) {
    parent::__construct(groups: $groups, payload: $payload);
}

  1. Remove getDefaultOption() and getRequiredOptions() overrides — these methods no longer exist on the Symfony 8 Constraint base class and must be deleted from any subclass.

  2. Update call sites that passed constraint options as a positional array (the old Symfony style):

// Before — passing options array as first argument
new MyConstraint(['myOption' => 'value']);

// After — pass named arguments directly
new MyConstraint(myOption: 'value');

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: January 22, 2026
  • Committed: May 5, 2026 (3 months later)

Key contributors

  • godotislate
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3581569: Remove user_cookie_save() and user_cookie_delete()
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +25/−2 · php +25/−2
  • Diff: 57bf4fe
  • Discussion: #3581569 · 5 contributors · 15 comments
  • Followers: 6

Summary

The two procedural cookie helpers user_cookie_save() and user_cookie_delete() are deprecated as of Drupal 11.4 and will be removed in Drupal 13.0. There is no direct replacement: callers should set or clear cookies directly on the Symfony Response object. The single core call site in SystemController::compactPage() has already been updated.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • Deprecation: user_cookie_save() and user_cookie_delete() deprecated in 11.4, removed in 13.0; set or clear cookies directly on the Symfony Response object instead.

Technical details

Both functions live in user.module. user_cookie_save() was used once in core, in SystemController::compactPage() to toggle the admin compact-mode cookie. user_cookie_delete() had no core callers at all. Both now emit E_USER_DEPRECATED via @trigger_error() and carry standard deprecation docblocks pointing to the change record at https://www.drupal.org/node/3581570.

The core call site in SystemController::compactPage() was rewritten to construct a Symfony Cookie object directly (setting a one-year expiry via $this->time->getRequestTime() + 31536000 when $mode === 'on', or calling $response->headers->clearCookie() otherwise). To support this, SystemController gains a new autowired constructor parameter readonly protected TimeInterface $time, with the corresponding use imports for TimeInterface and Cookie.

Upgrade

Replace user_cookie_save() by attaching a Cookie to the response headers:

// Before
user_cookie_save(['my_setting' => $value]);

// After (inside a controller that has access to the response)
use Symfony\Component\HttpFoundation\Cookie;

$response->headers->setCookie(
  new Cookie('Drupal.visitor.my_setting', $value, \Drupal::time()->getRequestTime() + 31536000)
);

Replace user_cookie_delete() by clearing the cookie on the response headers:

// Before
user_cookie_delete('my_setting');

// After
$response->headers->clearCookie('Drupal.visitor.my_setting');

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 26, 2026
  • First commit: April 13, 2026 (18 days later)
  • Last commit: May 4, 2026 (21 days and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • znerol
  • catch (Third and Grove)
  • godotislate

Collaboration

Reviewer dcam noted during the initial MR review that the change record demonstrated replacing user_cookie_delete() only implicitly, and pointed to several contrib uses of the function, suggesting the committer consider adding an explicit delete example to the change record before merging.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3585708: Preload fonts in admin theme
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 1 file, +3/−0 · yml +3
  • Diff: 3f4c757
  • Discussion: #3585708 · 4 contributors · 11 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

The Drupal admin theme now preloads the Inter font. By declaring the font with preload: true, the browser fetches it early in the page load sequence, reducing the flash of unstyled text (FOUT) that can appear in the administration UI before the font arrives.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The change adds a fonts key to the global-styling library definition in the admin theme's .libraries.yml file. The single entry, font/inter.woff2: {preload: true}, instructs the libraries API to emit an HTML <link rel="preload"> tag for the Inter font. This is a direct application of the font-preloading support introduced in #3366561, which added a first-class fonts key to the libraries API alongside the existing css and js keys. Inter is the only font used by the admin theme, so this single entry covers the full font set.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 20, 2026
  • Committed: May 4, 2026 (14 days and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • mukeysh
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3191278: Use plugin id to make help section headers HTML IDs
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 8 files, +16/−8 · php +8/−4 · twig +8/−4
  • Diff: 06c1f1b
  • Discussion: #3191278 · 8 contributors · 41 comments
  • Followers: 10

Summary

The section headings on the /admin/help page now carry HTML id attributes derived from each help section's plugin ID. This means sections can be linked to directly using URL fragments, for example /admin/help#help-topics or /admin/help#hook-help. The "See the Help page" link shown at the top of individual help-topic pages was also updated to anchor directly to the #help-topics section.

Impact

Affects front-end developers.

  • Theme change: The help_section theme hook gains a plugin_id variable; help-section.html.twig (and its overrides in Claro, Stable9, and Umami) now render <h2 id="{{ plugin_id|clean_id }}"> instead of a bare <h2>. Custom theme overrides of this template should add the id attribute accordingly.

Technical details

In HelpController::helpMain(), the plugin ID is now passed to the help_section render array as #plugin_id. The help_section theme hook definition (declared in the same controller's theme() method) gains plugin_id as a new variable defaulting to NULL. The help-section.html.twig template was updated in four locations: the canonical template in the Help module, Claro, Stable9, and the Umami installation profile. Each now renders <h2 id="{{ plugin_id|clean_id }}"> instead of a bare <h2>. The clean_id Twig filter converts underscores to hyphens and strips characters that are invalid in HTML id values, so hook_help becomes hook-help and help_topics becomes help-topics. The help.help_topic route handler's back-link was updated to call ->setOption('fragment', 'help-topics') on the URL so it lands on the correct anchor. Test assertions in HelpTest were updated to expect the new id attributes on the <h2> elements.

Upgrade

If your custom theme overrides help-section.html.twig, update the <h2> element to include the new id attribute:

{# Before #}
<h2>{{ title }}</h2>

{# After #}
<h2 id="{{ plugin_id|clean_id }}">{{ title }}</h2>

The plugin_id variable is now available in all help_section template contexts.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: January 6, 2021
  • Committed: May 3, 2026 (5 years and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • andypost (Skilld)
  • amber himes matz (Drupalize.Me)
  • tobiasb (Gesellschaft zur Entwicklung von Dingen)
  • rkoller
  • benjifisher (Harvard Web Publishing)
  • simohell (Druid)

Collaboration

andypost opened the issue in early 2021 with a working-in-progress patch but the approach needed refinement. Years later, amber himes matz proposed the concrete design: filter the plugin ID through clean_id for consistency with the hyphenated HTML ID convention already used elsewhere in the admin UI, and expose plugin_id through a preprocess step rather than calling plugin methods directly in the template. amber himes matz then created the MR, adapting andypost's original patch to the current codebase (where hooks had since moved out of .module files). andypost reviewed and asked that plugin_id be passed as a plain template variable rather than surfacing the plugin object, and tobiasb implemented that revision. amber himes matz then fixed additional failing test assertions before the MR was committed.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3530640: Disable the user.login.http route by default and move to REST
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 13 files, +486/−4 · php +432/−4 · yml +54
  • Diff: c0e71ef
  • Discussion: #3530640 · 12 contributors · 62 comments
  • Followers: 16

Summary

Starting in Drupal 11.4, the HTTP REST authentication endpoints (login, logout, login status, and password reset) require the rest module to be enabled. Previously these routes were part of the user module and always active, which meant third-party modules adding login protection (such as OTP or two-factor authentication) often secured the form login but left the REST endpoint unguarded. The URL paths (/user/login?_format=json, /user/logout, /user/login_status, /user/password) are unchanged, so API clients do not need to update their requests. Any Drupal code that generates URLs using the old route names (user.login.http, user.logout.http, user.login_status.http, user.pass.http) needs to be updated to the new names in the rest module.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and site owners.

  • Security: REST login endpoints are now disabled by default on sites without the rest module enabled, reducing exposure when third-party login-protection modules do not cover the REST route.
  • Requirements: The rest module must be enabled for HTTP REST authentication endpoints to function without deprecation warnings; previously only the user module was required.
  • API change: Routes user.login.http, user.logout.http, user.login_status.http, user.pass.http deprecated in 11.4.0 and removed in 12.0.0; use rest.login, rest.logout, rest.login_status, rest.pass respectively.
  • Deprecation: UserAuthenticationController deprecated in 11.4.0, removed in 12.0.0; use RestAuthenticationController in the rest module instead.

Technical details

The four HTTP authentication routes previously defined in user.routing.yml are deprecated in 11.4.0 and will be removed in 12.0.0. Equivalent routes with new names (rest.login, rest.logout, rest.login_status, rest.pass) are added to a new rest.routing.yml section with identical paths, so the HTTP URLs remain stable.

A new RestAuthenticationController in Drupal\rest\Controller holds all the authentication logic (login, logout, loginStatus, resetPassword), copied from the now-deprecated UserAuthenticationController. The old UserAuthenticationController fires a runtime E_USER_DEPRECATED from its constructor. The old routes in user.routing.yml carry deprecated keys pointing to the change record at https://www.drupal.org/node/3552724.

The path-preservation mechanism is a DeprecatedUserRoutesSubscriber event subscriber (registered as an autowired service in rest.services.yml) that listens on RoutingEvents::ALTER. When the rest module is installed, it replaces the _controller default on the four user module routes with RestAuthenticationController, so those routes no longer trigger the deprecation. If the rest module is not installed, the user routes remain active but instantiate the deprecated controller. The rest module also registers the four new route names with the existing format-negotiation event subscriber so _format=json continues to work on all of them. On the 11.x backport, RestAuthenticationController additionally handles the still-supported UserAuthInterface alongside UserAuthenticationInterface (which deprecates the former), matching the dual-path already present in UserAuthenticationController.

Upgrade

Enable the rest module if you use the REST login endpoints:

drush en rest

Update any Drupal code that generates URLs to these routes:

// Before (deprecated in 11.4, removed in 12.0)
Url::fromRoute('user.login.http', ['_format' => 'json']);
Url::fromRoute('user.logout.http', ['_format' => 'json']);
Url::fromRoute('user.login_status.http', ['_format' => 'json']);
Url::fromRoute('user.pass.http', ['_format' => 'json']);

// After
Url::fromRoute('rest.login', ['_format' => 'json']);
Url::fromRoute('rest.logout', ['_format' => 'json']);
Url::fromRoute('rest.login_status', ['_format' => 'json']);
Url::fromRoute('rest.pass', ['_format' => 'json']);

The URL paths (/user/login, /user/logout, etc.) are unchanged. No changes to external HTTP clients are required as long as the rest module is enabled.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 17, 2025
  • First commit: April 27, 2026 (10 months later)
  • Last commit: April 30, 2026 (3 days and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • prudloff (Insite)
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)
  • catch (Third and Grove)
  • mstrelan
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)

Collaboration

longwave drove the key design decision that resolved a significant disagreement in the thread. The initial MR used different paths for the new rest module routes, which would have forced all API clients to update their URLs. After pushback, longwave implemented the path-preservation approach (option #19 in the thread): keeping the old user.routing.yml routes at the same paths but using DeprecatedUserRoutesSubscriber to swap in the non-deprecated controller when rest is installed. longwave also handled the 11.x backport and caught a non-trivial compatibility issue: the backported RestAuthenticationController needed to support both UserAuthInterface and UserAuthenticationInterface because Drupal 11 still allows both, unlike main.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3533299: Deprecate node access rebuild functions
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 39 files, +346/−202 · php +345/−202 · yml +1
  • Diff: 3a0d690
  • Discussion: #3533299 · 7 contributors · 56 comments
  • Followers: 11

Summary

Four procedural functions for rebuilding node access grants have been replaced by a new NodeAccessRebuild service in the node module. node_access_needs_rebuild() and node_access_rebuild() are now deprecated and will be removed in Drupal 13. Any custom module or theme calling these functions should switch to the service. The two internal underscore-prefixed helpers (_node_access_rebuild_batch_operation and _node_access_rebuild_batch_finished) are removed immediately without deprecation.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • Deprecation: node_access_needs_rebuild() and node_access_rebuild() deprecated in 11.4.0 and removed in 13.0.0; use NodeAccessRebuild service methods instead. _node_access_rebuild_batch_operation() and _node_access_rebuild_batch_finished() removed immediately.
  • New API: New Drupal\node\NodeAccessRebuild service with needsRebuild(), setNeedsRebuild(), rebuild(), batchOperation(), and batchFinished() methods.

Technical details

The new Drupal\node\NodeAccessRebuild class encapsulates all node access rebuild logic and is registered as an autowired service in node.services.yml. It exposes three public methods: needsRebuild(), setNeedsRebuild(bool $rebuild = TRUE), and rebuild(bool $batch_mode = FALSE). The state key 'node.node_access_needs_rebuild' is preserved and exposed as NodeAccessRebuild::STATE_KEY. Batch processing logic previously in _node_access_rebuild_batch_operation() and _node_access_rebuild_batch_finished() is moved into batchOperation() and batchFinished() instance methods on the service. These are registered with BatchBuilder using the ClassName:methodName string callable format, which allows the batch system to instantiate the service from the container and use dependency injection rather than static calls. This depends on the callable type removal from BatchBuilder landed in a companion issue (#3575096).

RebuildPermissionsForm, NodeHelpHooks, and NodeModuleHooks all now receive NodeAccessRebuild via constructor injection and call its methods directly instead of the procedural functions. The deprecated node_access_needs_rebuild() and node_access_rebuild() functions now delegate to the service and emit E_USER_DEPRECATED errors; both are deprecated as of drupal:11.4.0 and scheduled for removal in drupal:13.0.0.

Upgrade

// Before (deprecated in 11.4.0, removed in 13.0.0):
node_access_needs_rebuild();
node_access_needs_rebuild(TRUE);
node_access_needs_rebuild(FALSE);
node_access_rebuild();
node_access_rebuild(TRUE);

// After:
use Drupal\node\NodeAccessRebuild;

$service = \Drupal::service(NodeAccessRebuild::class);

$service->needsRebuild();
$service->setNeedsRebuild(TRUE);
$service->setNeedsRebuild(FALSE);
$service->rebuild();
$service->rebuild(TRUE);

In injected classes, add NodeAccessRebuild to the constructor:

public function __construct(
  protected readonly NodeAccessRebuild $nodeAccessRebuild,
) {}

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 30, 2025
  • Committed: April 30, 2026 (10 months and 4 commits later)

Key contributors

  • acbramley (PreviousNext)
  • virag.jain (QED42)
  • mstrelan (PreviousNext)
  • dcam
  • nicxvan (nLightened Development LLC)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)

Collaboration

nicxvan pushed back twice on design details that shaped the final architecture. The first time (#16450339) nicxvan flagged that private properties would cause serialization issues in the batch context and that the batch callbacks should be non-static to allow dependency injection, but then held the issue open (#16500136) pointing to a companion change (#3575096) that needed to land first to enable non-static BatchBuilder callbacks. When acbramley submitted again (#16525877) with the batch methods still static, nicxvan kicked it back once more (#16525935), explaining that avoiding static methods was exactly the reason they had waited for #3575096. After acbramley converted the methods to instance methods, nicxvan approved. alexpott then committed the patch but immediately reverted it (#16571756) after noticing that some code comments in documentation still referenced node_access_rebuild() by name. alexpott fixed those references and committed a clean version.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3586606: Improve performance of HelpTopicsSyntaxTest
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 6 files, +178/−272 · php +178/−260 · yml +0/−12
  • Diff: 815e244
  • Discussion: #3586606 · 5 contributors · 28 comments
  • Followers: 9

Summary

HelpTopicsSyntaxTest was taking over 19 minutes to run in CI because it was a full functional (browser) test that repeatedly read and wrote State storage through a helper Twig extension. The test has been converted to a kernel test and the #slow group tag removed; it now completes in roughly 9 to 12 seconds.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The test previously extended BrowserTestBase and depended on a dedicated test support module, help_topics_twig_tester, which provided HelpTestTwigExtension and HelpTestTwigNodeVisitor. The node visitor tracked rendering state (which processing mode to apply, current chunk counts) by reading and writing \Drupal::state() on every Twig node traversal. That pattern meant dozens of database round-trips per topic render, and the problem was amplified further by the pending changes in #3496257.

The fix moves the test to KernelTestBase and implements NodeVisitorInterface directly on HelpTopicsSyntaxTest itself. State is now tracked in plain PHP properties: $this->manner, $this->maxChunk, and $this->returnChunk. The Twig extension is registered inline via an anonymous class inside register(ContainerBuilder $container), injecting $this as the node visitor. The entire help_topics_twig_tester module is deleted. The drupalGet() page-load calls inside verifyTopic() are also removed, since HelpTopicTest::verifyHelp() already provides a smoke test of the controller. A stale PHPStan $ignoreErrors entry for HelpTestTwigNodeVisitor::setStateValue() is also cleaned up. The simplification during merge also collapsed the separate chunk_count and max_chunk tracking into a single $this->maxChunk counter incremented inline in leaveNode().

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 23, 2026
  • Committed: April 29, 2026 (5 days and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • godotislate
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)
  • catch (Third and Grove)

Collaboration

longwave opened a parallel MR (!15577) with the observation that the drupalGet() calls were unnecessary overhead given that HelpTopicTest::verifyHelp() already smoke-tests the controller, and that dropping them would make the diff easier to read. godotislate agreed, and the two MRs were merged into one. longwave went further during the merge, refactoring the chunk tracking variables and noting that max_chunk and chunk_count were redundant, collapsing them into a single counter.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3502993: Convert Navigation messages component to SDC
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 8 files, +48/−38 · yml +32/−9 · css +8/−2 · php +6/−13 · twig +2/−14
  • Diff: 6923cfc
  • Discussion: #3502993 · 8 contributors · 38 comments
  • Followers: 10

Summary

The Navigation module's message display element has been converted from a Twig theme hook to a Single Directory Component (SDC). The old navigation__message theme hook is removed; any code that was rendering messages via #theme => 'navigation__message' must now use the navigation:message SDC component instead. The CSS that was previously declared in a standalone internal.navigation-messages library is now bundled inside the SDC, so that library is also gone. As a visual fix, the pointer cursor is now only applied when a message element actually has an href attribute, meaning non-linked messages no longer render with a pointer cursor.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • API change: The navigation__message theme hook is removed; callers must render the navigation:message SDC component using #type => 'component' instead.
  • Deprecation: The internal.navigation-messages CSS library is removed; remove any dependencies on it.

Technical details

The navigation__message theme hook previously defined in NavigationThemeHooks::theme() is removed. In its place, a new SDC component navigation:message is introduced, defined in a .component.yml file with three typed string props: type (enum error | status | warning, default status), url (format iri-reference, optional), and content (plain string). The component's Twig template uses {% if url %} to conditionally render either a link <a> or a static <div>.

The internal.navigation-messages CSS library entry is removed from navigation.libraries.yml, and the corresponding {{ attach_library(...) }} call is dropped from navigation__messages.html.twig, because the component's styles are now declared and scoped inside the SDC itself. The BEM modifier class is corrected from toolbar-message--type--{type} to toolbar-message--{type}, and cursor: pointer is moved from the base .toolbar-message rule to a new .toolbar-message[href] attribute selector so it only applies to linked messages.

The Umami integration in NavigationContentTopBlock::navigationContentTop() is updated to use #type => 'component' and #component => 'navigation:message' with a #props array, replacing the old #theme => 'navigation__message' render array. This is the reference implementation for how any other module or profile should render a Navigation message going forward.

Upgrade

Any code rendering a Navigation message via the old theme hook must switch to the SDC component:

// Before
$element = [
  '#theme' => 'navigation__message',
  '#content' => ['#markup' => $this->t('Some message.')],
  '#url' => $url,
  '#type' => 'warning',
];

// After
$element = [
  '#type' => 'component',
  '#component' => 'navigation:message',
  '#props' => [
    'type' => 'warning',
    'url' => $url,
    'content' => (string) $this->t('Some message.'),
  ],
];

Any library dependency on navigation/internal.navigation-messages should also be removed, as that library no longer exists.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: January 29, 2025
  • Committed: April 29, 2026 (1 year and 4 commits later)

Key contributors

  • plopesc (Lullabot)
  • nod_ (Très Bien Tech)
  • finnsky (Skilld)
  • pdureau
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)

Collaboration

pdureau provided detailed design review early in the thread, catching several SDC authoring issues: slots must not carry a type property, the BEM class name had a redundant --type-- segment, and using a slot value as a data-drupal-tooltip attribute value is unsafe because slots can contain markup. He recommended making content a typed string prop and injecting tooltip attributes from the caller. These suggestions directly shaped the final component definition. Later, alexpott flagged that removing an item from NavigationThemeHooks::theme() is a BC break that needs a deprecation or change record, prompting a Slack discussion that concluded with the theme hook being removed outright and a change record added.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3586616: Remove search from olivero_test module
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 5 files, +82/−14 · js +46/−13 · php +36 · yml +0/−1
  • Diff: cb1f6e0
  • Discussion: #3586616 · 4 contributors · 13 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

The olivero_test testing module no longer declares a hard dependency on the Search module. Search-related Nightwatch test setup and tests that were previously tied to olivero_test have been moved into the Search module's own test directory. This is an internal test infrastructure change with no effect on production sites, but it unblocks a future deprecation of the core Search module.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The olivero_test module listed drupal:search as a dependency in olivero_test.info.yml solely to support Nightwatch browser tests that verified Olivero's search form blocks. Removing that dependency required extracting the search-specific test concerns into the Search module itself.

The fix adds a new TestSiteOliveroInstallTestScript class under core/modules/search/tests/src/Nightwatch/Olivero/ (implementing TestSetupInterface) that installs both olivero_test and search and sets Olivero as the default theme. A new Nightwatch test file placed under core/modules/search/tests/src/Nightwatch/Tests/Olivero/ handles disabling the narrow and wide Olivero search form blocks, which was the search-specific setup previously embedded in oliveroRemoveBlockConsoleTest.js. That existing test (in core/tests/Drupal/Nightwatch/) was updated to reference the new setup script path and had its search block interaction steps removed.

During review, dcam observed that the new Nightwatch JS files were not actually being picked up by the test runner because they were not inside a Tests/ subdirectory, matching the convention used by ckeditor, navigation, and toolbar modules. The files were corrected to follow that convention. alexpott backported the change to the 11.x branch explicitly to allow the Search module to be deprecated there.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 24, 2026
  • Committed: April 29, 2026 (5 days and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • quietone (PreviousNext)
  • dcam
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)

Collaboration

dcam caught that the newly added Nightwatch test files were not running because they were placed outside the expected Tests/ subdirectory. This observation, backed by a specific review of the CI test output, prompted the path correction that made the tests actually execute.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3578444: Date filter doesn't apply default offset values
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 11.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +86/−0 · php +86
  • Diff: ec09b79
  • Discussion: #3578444 · 4 contributors · 15 comments
  • Followers: 6

Summary

Views exposed date filters configured with a default "offset" value (such as "now" or "+2 days") were silently ignored on initial page load. When a visitor arrived at the view without submitting the exposed filter form, the filter produced no results restriction, effectively showing all content regardless of the configured date default. This is now fixed: the default offset is applied automatically so the view filters correctly from the first page load.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The bug was in acceptExposedInput() inside the Views date filter handler (core/modules/views/src/Plugin/views/filter/Date.php). When the exposed input arrived empty, the method immediately returned FALSE for both the single-value operator path and the between/not-between operator path, bypassing the filter entirely. It never checked whether a default offset had been configured in $this->options['value'].

The fix inserts a check just before each early return FALSE. If the submitted value is blank, the code now inspects $this->options['value']['type']. When that equals 'offset' and the configured default value is non-empty, the method copies the stored offset (either value for single-value operators, or min/max for range operators) into $this->value, sets $this->value['type'] to 'offset', and returns TRUE so the filter proceeds to query the backend with the resolved date. If no default offset exists the original return FALSE path is preserved.

Two functional tests were added to testExposedOffsetDefaultAppliedOnPageLoad() and testExposedOffsetBetweenDefaultAppliedOnPageLoad(), covering the single-value > operator case and the between operator case respectively, including a node placed beyond the upper bound to confirm it is correctly excluded.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 11, 2026
  • Committed: April 29, 2026 (1 month and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • macsim
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • lendude (iO)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3578374: Autowire core modules that require explicit configuration
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: main
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 57 files, +282/−1236 · php +272/−1152 · yml +10/−84
  • Diff: a24f668
  • Discussion: #3578374 · 8 contributors · 20 comments
  • Followers: 8

Summary

A batch of Drupal core service classes across many modules has been converted to use Symfony's #[Autowire] attribute for dependency injection, removing the corresponding explicit arguments: arrays from their .services.yml definitions. Several service files also gained autowire: true in their _defaults block. This is a purely internal refactoring with no user-facing behavior change.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The predecessor issue #3397041 autowired core services whose constructor parameter types were sufficient for Symfony to resolve the dependency unambiguously. This issue covers the remaining cases where type alone is ambiguous: for example, a constructor that accepts CacheBackendInterface could legally receive cache.default, cache.discovery, or cache.data, and a RouterInterface parameter might need router.no_access_checks rather than the standard router. For those parameters, #[Autowire(service: 'explicit.service.id')] attributes are added directly on the constructor parameter.

Affected classes include LayoutPluginManager, BigPipeResponseAttachmentsProcessor, BlockContentUuidLookup, BreakpointManager, SmartDefaultSettings (ckeditor5), ConfigMapperManager, editor\Element, FilterUninstallValidator, EntityAccessChecker, EntityResource, ResourceResponseSubscriber and ResourceObjectNormalizer (jsonapi), ResourceTypeRepository, LanguageNegotiationMethodManager, LanguageNegotiator, LocaleConfigManager, LocaleTranslation, media\OEmbed\ResourceFetcher, media\OEmbed\UrlResolver, navigation\EntityRouteHelper, path_alias\AliasManager, rest\ResourcePluginManager, and rest\ResourceResponseSubscriber, among others.

As a secondary change within the same commit, classes that previously assigned injected services to instance properties inside the constructor body were converted to constructor property promotion, and the associated @var docblocks and constructor @param docblocks were removed. The service YAML arguments: arrays for all affected services were dropped, and autowire: true was added to the _defaults block of several modules' service files (breakpoint, language, layout_discovery, responsive_image). The notation for autowired-only service aliases (e.g., Drupal\ckeditor5\LanguageMapper: ~ replacing autowire: true) was also normalized.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 10, 2026
  • Committed: April 29, 2026 (1 month later)

Key contributors

  • dcam
  • godotislate
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)

Collaboration

dcam opened the MR and rebased it multiple times as other work landed on main. During review, godotislate raised the question of whether adding constructor property promotion was in scope given the increased diff size. After a Slack discussion between dcam and godotislate, dcam agreed to implement it and added that conversion to the MR. smustgrave reviewed and approved. alexpott committed the result.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3567230: Only overwrite mapped field values on media items when source field original value was non-empty
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 11.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +45/−3 · php +45/−3
  • Diff: 57d2fe4
  • Discussion: #3567230 · 4 contributors · 22 comments
  • Followers: 8

Summary

When saving a media entity, Drupal extracts metadata from the source plugin and writes it into any fields configured in the media type field map. A bug caused those mapped fields to be silently overwritten if a media item was first saved without a source field value and then later saved again with one, even when the mapped fields already held legitimate values. Sites that programmatically create media entities in two passes, or that use tools such as Entity Share to import media, could see mapped field values (captions, alt text, and similar) replaced with raw plugin metadata. This fix tightens the condition so that existing values in mapped fields are only replaced when the source field transitions between two non-empty values.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The metadata-mapping loop in Media::prepareSave() previously allowed two write paths: write if the mapped field is empty, or write if hasSourceFieldChanged() returned TRUE. Because hasSourceFieldChanged() compares the original and current source field values, it returns TRUE whenever the original was NULL, including the case where the entity simply had no source file on its first save. That made the condition too broad.

The fix introduces a third, stricter condition for the non-empty-field case. $this->getOriginal() is called once before the field-map loop. Inside the loop, after confirming the mapped field is not empty, two additional checks are performed: $media_source->getSourceFieldValue($original) === NULL to detect an empty original source, and $media_source->getSourceFieldValue($translation) === NULL to detect an empty new source. Metadata is now only written when both source values are non-null and hasSourceFieldChanged() also returns TRUE. The === NULL comparison (rather than empty()) was chosen deliberately because getSourceFieldValue() already returns NULL for any empty field item, and 0 is a legitimate non-empty field value that empty() would incorrectly treat as absent.

The related issue #2999370 covers a similar overwrite problem in the translation-save code path and remains open.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: January 13, 2026
  • Committed: April 29, 2026 (3 months and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • marcoscano (Lullabot)
  • anybody (DROWL.de)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)

Collaboration

alexpott flagged during review that the initial patch used empty() to test the original source field value, which would incorrectly treat 0 as empty. He read through MediaSourceBase::getSourceFieldValue() and the isEmpty() implementations for string and numeric field items, confirmed that getSourceFieldValue() always returns NULL for empty fields and never for 0, and proposed replacing empty() with a strict === NULL check. The contributor applied the suggestion before alexpott committed the result.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #2907780: Add a field purger service
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 24 files, +329/−175 · php +311/−161 · yml +18/−14
  • Diff: 867318c
  • Discussion: #2907780 · 20 contributors · 89 comments
  • Followers: 36

Summary

The procedural field-purging functions in the Field module are deprecated in Drupal 11.2 and replaced by a new entity_field.purger service living in core's Entity Field API. The purge_batch_size setting is also moved from the field.settings configuration object to system.entity_field_settings, with an automatic database update to migrate existing values. Cron-triggered purging moves from the Field module to the System module. For most site owners the change is transparent: the update hook handles the config migration and purging continues to run on cron as before.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and site owners.

  • Database update: system_update_11103() migrates purge_batch_size from field.settings to system.entity_field_settings and deletes the old config object.
  • Config change: field.settings config object removed; purge_batch_size moved to system.entity_field_settings.
  • Deprecation: field_purge_batch(), field_purge_field(), and field_purge_field_storage() deprecated in drupal:11.2.0 and removed in drupal:12.0.0; use FieldPurger::purgeBatch(), purgeFieldDefinition(), and purgeFieldStorageDefinition() respectively.
  • New API: New entity_field.purger service (FieldPurger implementing FieldPurgerInterface) for purging deleted field data, registered in core.

Technical details

Field purging logic had always lived in the Field module's field_purge.inc as three standalone procedural functions (field_purge_batch(), field_purge_field(), field_purge_field_storage()), making it inaccessible to other parts of the Entity Field API that need to purge base fields and bundle fields outside the configurable-field system. The fix introduces core/lib/Drupal/Core/Field/FieldPurger.php, implementing the new FieldPurgerInterface, and registers it as the entity_field.purger service (with Drupal\Core\Field\FieldPurgerInterface as an alias in core.services.yml). FieldPurger is injected with EntityTypeManagerInterface, DeletedFieldsRepositoryInterface, ModuleHandlerInterface, and LoggerChannelFactoryInterface. The three procedural functions in field_purge.inc are reduced to thin wrappers that emit an E_USER_DEPRECATED trigger and forward to the service; they are deprecated in drupal:11.2.0 and scheduled for removal in drupal:12.0.0. FieldPurger::purgeFieldDefinition() and FieldPurger::purgeFieldStorageDefinition() are marked protected so subclasses can override the per-item logic while the public purgeBatch() remains the intended entry point. The Field module's #[Hook('cron')] implementation that called field_purge_batch() was removed entirely; equivalent logic was added to the System module's SystemHooks::cron(), reading the batch size from the new system.entity_field_settings config key. The field.settings config object is removed from Field module config; system_update_11103() copies purge_batch_size to system.entity_field_settings and deletes the old object. ConfigImporterFieldPurger was updated to read purge_batch_size from system.entity_field_settings. FieldConfigDeleteForm in field_ui was updated to inject FieldPurgerInterface via the service container instead of calling the procedural function. Tests throughout core were updated to use \Drupal::service('entity_field.purger') directly. A follow-up issue (#3587460) tracks fully removing the now-empty field.settings config object in Drupal 13. The migration pattern for config-to-settings follows the approach established in #3571593.

Upgrade

// Before (deprecated in drupal:11.2.0, removed in drupal:12.0.0):
field_purge_batch($batch_size);
field_purge_field($field);
field_purge_field_storage($field_storage);

// After — use the service directly or inject FieldPurgerInterface:
\Drupal::service('entity_field.purger')->purgeBatch($batch_size);
\Drupal::service('entity_field.purger')->purgeFieldDefinition($field);
\Drupal::service('entity_field.purger')->purgeFieldStorageDefinition($field_storage);

If your code reads field.settings:purge_batch_size from configuration, update it to read system.entity_field_settings:purge_batch_size. The system_update_11103() update hook migrates the value automatically for existing sites.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: September 9, 2017
  • Committed: April 29, 2026 (8 years and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • amateescu
  • plach (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)
  • anmolgoyal74 (OpenSense Labs)
  • nicxvan
  • berdir (MD Systems GmbH)
  • claudiu.cristea (Webikon)
  • andypost (Skilld)
  • larowlan (PreviousNext)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)

Collaboration

The issue was opened in 2017 by amateescu, who wrote an initial "field purgatory" class and spent years keeping it unblocked on prerequisite issues. After bouncing through every major release branch without landing, catch's 2024 comment that the patch needed updating for OOP hooks prompted anmolgoyal74 to re-roll it at DrupalCon Singapore 2024. amateescu then returned, renamed the service to "field purger," and brought the patch fully up to current core standards, including writing the change record. nicxvan took the final leg: rebasing on main, converting the config migration to follow the pattern from #3571593, addressing nits from claudiu.cristea, larowlan, and alexpott, and seeing it through to commit by alexpott.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.
